assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12 shows 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 83-93 detail each criterion: (1) single license filter PASS, (2) comma-separated license filter PASS, (3) invalid license returns 400 PASS, (4) pagination integration PASS, (5) response shape unchanged PASS. Criterion files criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Verdict: PASS' with detailed analysis.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7 shows 'Scope Containment | PASS | All 3 files match task specification exactly (2 modified, 1 created)'. Lines 25-36 provide file-by-file comparison: PR diff files (list.rs modified, service/mod.rs modified, package.rs new) match exactly against task specification (Files to Modify: list.rs, service/mod.rs; Files to Create: tests/api/package.rs). Line 36: 'No out-of-scope files, no unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5 shows 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in the outputs. The report does not contain any evidence of Jira sub-task creation actions.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md — all are verification/analysis documents. None of the output files contain evidence of file edits, git commits, or code modifications. The report only references reading and analyzing diff content (e.g., 'All added lines in the diff were scanned', 'Code changes supporting this criterion').

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and 'All checks pass. The implementation correctly satisfies all 5 acceptance criteria with appropriate test coverage.' There is no merge action, no gh pr merge command, and no indication of auto-merge. The report presents findings as a verification report for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10 shows 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Lines 55-72 detail the scan: patterns checked include 'Hardcoded passwords and secrets', 'API keys and tokens', 'Private keys and certificates', 'Environment and configuration files', 'Cloud provider credentials', 'Database credentials'. Result: 'No matches found.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14 shows 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | 1 new test file with 4 test functions; no modified or deleted test files'. Lines 128-130 elaborate: 'tests/api/package.rs is a new file (listed in "Files to Create" in the task specification). It adds 4 test functions with 10+ assertions. No test files were modified or deleted. Classification: ADDITIVE.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table (lines 5-15) contains all expected rows: Review Feedback, Root-Cause Investigation, Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability, Sensitive Patterns, CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Test Quality (with 'Eval Quality: N/A' noted), Test Change Classification, Verification Commands. The detailed sections are organized by domain: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 24-49) covers Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability; 'Security' (lines 53-72) covers Sensitive Pattern Scan; 'Correctness' (lines 76-97) covers CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands; 'Style/Conventions' (lines 101-130) covers Convention Upgrade, Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, Test Change Classification. Test Quality in the summary combines Repetitive Test Detection (PASS), Test Documentation (PASS), and Eval Quality (N/A).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 25-49): file-by-file comparison listing exact paths for PR diff files and task specification files, with diff size metrics (~100 lines added, ~3 deleted). Security (lines 57-72): lists 6 pattern categories scanned and describes diff content (import statements, struct fields, validation logic, query construction, test functions). Correctness (lines 83-93): per-criterion analysis citing specific code constructs (spdx::Expression::parse, is_in filter, Condition::any(), PaginatedResults type) and test function names with specific assertions. Style/Conventions (lines 107-130): names all 4 test functions with descriptions, explains why they are not parameterization candidates, quotes all 4 doc comment strings verbatim. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide multi-section analysis with code references and conclusions for each acceptance criterion.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13 shows 'Test Quality | PASS | All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected (Eval Quality: N/A)'. Line 125 shows 'Eval Quality -- N/A' with line 127: 'No eval result reviews found on this PR.' The Test Quality verdict is PASS based on Repetitive Test Detection (PASS) and Test Documentation (PASS), with Eval Quality explicitly excluded as N/A.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 125-127: 'Eval Quality -- N/A / No eval result reviews found on this PR.'). No sub-tasks of any kind are mentioned as created in the outputs. No Jira task creation actions appear anywhere in the report or criterion files.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6 show 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created'. These appear in the summary table before the domain-specific sections (Intent Alignment starts at line 22, Security at line 53, Correctness at line 76, Style/Conventions at line 101), indicating they are orchestrator-level verdicts separate from the four domain analyses.
